skills.name: answerer
skills.summary: Draft a reply to an inbound question, DM the user for approval, post on confirmation

skills.name: architecture-design
skills.summary: Architecture pre-check companion. Loaded transitively by implementation skills (code, ticket-for-features, retro-for-skill-changes) to force an architecture pass — BLUEPRINT alignment, FSM phase boundaries, extension-point contracts, component boundaries, dependency direction, test surface, resilience invariants — BEFORE any code is written

skills.name: availability
skills.summary: 24/7 dual question-mode — switch between asking the user now (present) and capturing questions as durable `DeferredQuestion` rows (away)

skills.name: backlog-sweep
skills.summary: Periodic evidence-gated triage of the issue backlog — for each open issue, classify it against current `main` as superseded / stale / regressive / still-valid, then propose close-with-citation. The retire counterpart to teatree-plan's prioritize, reusing its GitHub Projects board layer and one-decision-per-question walkthrough. Dry-run first; close only on user approval (or auto-close ONLY the high-confidence "superseded by merged PR #X" class), posting a one-line reason on every close

skills.name: checking
skills.summary: A SHORT "what did I miss" report when the user checks in mid-loop — terse, grouped, clickable; then answer the pending deferred questions in-band

skills.name: code
skills.summary: Writing code with TDD methodology

skills.name: contribute
skills.summary: Push retro improvements to a branch, open a PR, and optionally create upstream issues

skills.name: debug
skills.summary: Troubleshooting and fixing — something is broken, find and fix it

skills.name: e2e
skills.summary: End-to-end testing with Playwright — writing tests, running them, visual snapshots, test-plan posting, and the pre-push visual QA gate

skills.name: e2e-review
skills.summary: Reviewer-side quality gate for Playwright end-to-end specs. Load when reviewing a new or changed E2E test, deciding whether a spec is ready to land, or adopting an outside Playwright suite. Judges specs against Playwright's published best practices — user-visible behaviour over implementation, resilient role/label/test-id locators, web-first auto-retrying assertions instead of hard waits, per-test isolation, page-object structure, and runnable evidence — and tells the implementer what to fix before approval

skills.name: followup
skills.summary: Daily follow-up — batch process new tickets, check/advance ticket statuses, remind about PRs waiting for review

skills.name: handover
skills.summary: Use when the user wants to hand all current work from one Claude session to another (or to a not-yet-existing session) with a single command, or to transfer an in-flight TeaTree task from Claude to another runtime, or asks whether it is time to switch because Claude usage is getting high

skills.name: loops
skills.summary: Show t3 loop status and trigger DB-configured loops — which loops are running vs stalled, the cadence/next-tick of each, loop ownership, and how to trigger a master tick

skills.name: next
skills.summary: Wrap up the current session — retro, structured result, pipeline handoff

skills.name: platforms
skills.summary: Platform-specific API recipes for GitLab, GitHub, Slack, and X (Twitter). Auto-loaded as a dependency by skills that interact with these platforms

skills.name: prompts
skills.summary: Trigger and manage reusable prompts — list the prompts in the DB, render one by name with its templated params, and point to the admin for authoring + version history

skills.name: retro
skills.summary: Conversation retrospective and skill improvement

skills.name: review
skills.summary: Code review — self-review before finalization, giving review, receiving review feedback

skills.name: review-request
skills.summary: Batch review requests — discover open PRs, validate metadata, check for duplicates, post to review channels

skills.name: rules
skills.summary: Cross-cutting agent safety rules — clickable refs, temp files, sub-agent limits, UX preservation. Auto-loaded as a dependency by other skills

skills.name: running-evals
skills.summary: Single in-session entrypoint that auto-orchestrates the whole eval picture — free deterministic lanes (skill-triggers, pinned-regressions) plus the transcript AI/trajectory lane (prepare → produce transcripts in-session → grade) — and prints one unified results table

skills.name: scanning-news
skills.summary: Scans today's TLDR AI and The Rundown AI editions for ideas that could improve teatree, fetches the full article for promising items, queues each concrete t3-improvement candidate behind an ask-gate (PendingArticleSuggestion) for per-article user approval before any souliane/teatree issue is filed, and posts a terse Slack DM summary

skills.name: setup
skills.summary: Bootstrap and validate teatree for local use — prerequisites, config, skill symlinks, optional agent hooks, and Django project scaffolding

skills.name: ship
skills.summary: Delivery — committing, pushing, creating MR/PR, pipeline monitoring, review requests

skills.name: speed
skills.summary: The parallel-work throughput dial — slow / medium / full / boost. `boost` runs one parallel-backlog-blast wave; `full` arms a self-sustaining boost loop; `medium` (baseline) and `slow` cap concurrency

skills.name: sweeping-prs
skills.summary: Maintenance sweep across all your open PRs/PRs — merge the default branch, fix conflicts, monitor CI, push, and (per-repo policy) optionally squash-merge each PR before moving to the next. Never rebases

skills.name: teatree
skills.summary: TeaTree agent lifecycle platform — core architecture, lifecycle phases, CLI reference, overlay API, skill loading, and plugin hooks

skills.name: teatree-batch
skills.summary: Unattended batch ticket processing — work through a prioritized backlog one ticket at a time, sequentially. Create worktree, implement with TDD, self-review, push, merge, clean up. Skip tickets that need design decisions

skills.name: teatree-bughunt
skills.summary: Self-QA variant of batch mode — dogfood the teatree loop and statusline, find real bugs (missing signals, broken links, stale data, scanner errors), file them, then fix them in worktrees

skills.name: teatree-dogfood
skills.summary: Dogfooding checklist for teatree CLI, loop, and statusline changes — verify fresh behavior by running the command yourself, exercising the full task lifecycle, and watching the rendered statusline before declaring a change done. Also lists the known worktree/uv/git-stash pitfalls that trip up local validation

skills.name: teatree-plan
skills.summary: Backlog prioritization with the GitHub Projects v2 board as single source of truth. Syncs repo issues to the board, walks the user through prioritization one question at a time, and reorders/updates board columns

skills.name: test
skills.summary: Testing, QA, and CI — running tests, analyzing failures, quality checks, CI interaction, test plans, and posting testing evidence

skills.name: ticket
skills.summary: Ticket intake and kickoff — from zero to ready-to-code

skills.name: todos
skills.summary: List the current session's tasks/todos — terse, grouped pending / in_progress / completed, with clickable refs

skills.name: update
skills.summary: WHEN to bring teatree core and registered overlays up to date with their default branch, and the safety guarantees of doing so

skills.name: workspace
skills.summary: Environment and workspace lifecycle — worktree creation, setup, DB provisioning, dev servers, cleanup
